name: Benny Bailey
abstract: Ernest Harold "Benny" Bailey (August 13, 1925 – April 14, 2005) was an American jazz trumpeter.

concerts.label: Kool-Newport Jazz Festival: Tribute to Charlie Parker
Kool Jazz Festival: Musicians for Each Other

about_quotes.speaker: Slide Hampton
about_quotes.evidence: I played with Dexter Gordon, Johnny Griffin, Benny Bailey, oh just a host of guys that were living
about_quotes.doc_title: Slide Hampton interviewed by Monk Rowe, West Orange, New Jersey, July 30, 2003

about_quotes.speaker: Art Farmer
about_quotes.evidence: I played one a TV gala in Stuttgart, Benny Bailey and I... we played there on a jam session with Lionel. I told Benny Bailey...
about_quotes.doc_title: Art Farmer — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: David N. Baker
about_quotes.evidence: Freddie [Hubbard] was in the trumpet section along with Benny Bailey and a guy they picked up over there...
about_quotes.doc_title: David N. Baker — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Slide Hampton
about_quotes.evidence: That band, Joe Haider, we were co-leaders... Dexter was in that band, and Benny Bailey was in that band
about_quotes.doc_title: Slide Hampton — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Randy Weston
about_quotes.evidence: I had a ten-piece group of the great Benny Bailey, Sahib Shihab, TK Blue, Clyde Lucas on drums...
about_quotes.doc_title: Randy Weston — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Patricia Willard
about_quotes.evidence: Benny Bailey played a very beautiful solo all by himself on 'Out Of Nowhere.' ... 'Bird Legs,' which was Benny Bailey's composition. There was also a thing called 'R.V.'s Wig.'
